name: crash_disabled_ignores_other_controllers
code:
proof fn phi_1_crash_disabled_ignores_other_controllers(controller_id: int, other_id: int, s: ClusterState)
    requires
        controller_id != other_id,
        s.controller_and_externals.contains_key(controller_id),
        s.controller_and_externals.contains_key(other_id),
        Cluster::crash_disabled(controller_id)(s),
    ensures
        s.controller_and_externals[other_id].crash_enabled || !s.controller_and_externals[other_id].crash_enabled,
{
}
reason: crash_disabled only constrains a single controller_id — other controllers can still crash freely, meaning the "failure-free" assumption is only local and the system can still lose state from other controller crashes

name: schedule_spec_match_ignores_metadata_diff
reason: the_object_in_schedule_has_spec_and_uid_as only constrains spec and uid — labels, annotations, resource_version, and other metadata fields can differ between the scheduled object and the reference vrs, so reconciliation may operate on stale metadata

name: stable_always_external_body_unsound
reason: always_p_is_stable has #[verifier::external_body] so its postcondition is assumed without proof — if the implementation is incorrect, any temporal property wrapped in always() can be declared stable, enabling unsound stability chains

name: invariants_phase_i_no_etcd_constraint
reason: invariants_since_phase_i constrains failure modes and schedule consistency but places zero constraints on etcd contents — the resource store can be arbitrarily mutated by other controllers or API server transitions while these invariants hold

name: object_ref_collision_in_schedule
code:
proof fn phi_5_object_ref_collision_in_schedule(controller_id: int, vrs1: VReplicaSetView, vrs2: VReplicaSetView, s: ClusterState)
    requires
        vrs1.metadata().name == vrs2.metadata().name,
        vrs1.metadata().namespace == vrs2.metadata().namespace,
        vrs1.metadata().name is Some,
        vrs1.metadata().namespace is Some,
        vrs1.metadata().uid != vrs2.metadata().uid,
        vrs1.spec() != vrs2.spec(),
    ensures
        vrs1.object_ref() == vrs2.object_ref(),
{
}
reason: two VReplicaSetViews with different uids and different specs produce the same object_ref (kind+name+namespace), so the scheduled_reconciles map can hold at most one — if a delete-recreate occurs, the schedule silently loses the old incarnation's entry
